title: condition.v1 — unified condition node vocabulary
description: Single source of truth for the condition-node vocabulary shared by afb.deal.v2, afb.tradeplan.v2 and afb.alarm.v1. Left/right pairing is enforced per source via oneOf: price fully supports six operators, none deprecated — `touch` (op omitted, for wire back-compat, or op="touch"): level between prev/cur; `above`/`below`: inclusive level check (cur >= level / cur <= level), optionally with `duration` (seconds, BF-only debounce); `breakout`/`breakdown`/`crossing`: candle op, requires `timeframe`. BF executes a fired `touch` as a LIMIT order and the other five as a MARKET order — see belphegor.plan_engine.order_policy. Deprecated tick crosses_* on price/quote remain accepted for backward compatibility only. `field` is required only on dataset expressions; price and indicator legs omit it on the wire (legacy `field` on read is ignored). indicator and dataset nodes use strict scalar ops (above/below/crosses_*/crossing).

scalarOp.description: indicator/dataset: above cur > thr, below cur < thr. crosses_above: prev <= prev_thr AND cur > thr. crosses_below: prev >= prev_thr AND cur < thr. crossing: crosses_above OR crosses_below. Price above/below use evaluate_price_level_op (inclusive >= / <=) — see the price level operator branch.

priceLevelOp.description: Price level check (inclusive): above fires when cur >= level; below when cur <= level.

deprecatedTickScalarOp.description: deprecated (v1.4.0) tick-by-tick price/quote ops without timeframe. Removal planned for v2.0.0.

duration.description: Optional protective time in seconds: condition must hold for this many consecutive BF monitor ticks (~1 s each) before firing. BF executor only; AFB draft emulation and alarms ignore it.

quoteExpr.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): quote (bid/ask) conditions are still accepted and evaluated, but are no longer offered by the AFB UI and will be removed in v2.0.0.

indicatorExpr.description: `field` is optional and should not be sent on the wire; AFB resolves alarm indicators by `id`, BF by `type`+`params`.

datasetExpr.description: position.* / orders / hhi / trades datasets share this shape; dataset_id=volume is declared here but temporarily unsupported by AFB/BF backends (see condition_semantics module docstring) and not offered by the UI.

oneOf.title: price touch
oneOf.description: Level touched when min(prev, cur) <= level <= max(prev, cur). `op` may be omitted (accepted on read for wire back-compat with pre-touch-op deals/plans) or given explicitly as "touch" (what AFB now always sends). Executed by BF as a LIMIT order.

oneOf.title: price candle operator
oneOf.description: breakout: last CLOSED candle of `timeframe` has open < level AND close > level. breakdown: open > level AND close < level. crossing: breakout OR breakdown. Evaluated only on candle close, never intra-bar. Executed by BF as a MARKET order.

oneOf.title: price level operator
oneOf.description: Inclusive price level: above = cur >= level, below = cur <= level. Optional `duration` (seconds) for BF protective-time debounce. Executed by BF as a MARKET order.

oneOf.title: price tick operator (deprecated)
oneOf.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): tick-by-tick crosses_* / crossing without timeframe. Removal planned for v2.0.0.

oneOf.title: quote operator (deprecated)
oneOf.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): bid/ask conditions using the scalar vocabulary. Removal planned for v2.0.0.

oneOf.title: indicator operator
oneOf.description: Scalar comparison (see #/$defs/scalarOp) of an indicator against a constant or another indicator expression. `timeframe` is optional at the schema level (this same conditionNode is reused by afb.alarm.v1, where the timeframe lives on the alarm itself, not the node) but is REQUIRED by the BF executor for afb.deal.v2/afb.tradeplan.v2 indicator conditions — see condition_semantics module docstring. When present, it applies to BOTH sides of the comparison: left and right are evaluated on the same bar series. Indicator values are a (previous CLOSED bar, current FORMING bar) pair, matching evaluate_scalar_op's cur/prev semantics.

oneOf.title: dataset operator
oneOf.description: Scalar comparison (see #/$defs/scalarOp) of a dataset value (position.*, orders, hhi, trades) against a constant or another dataset expression of the same dataset_id.
